# Nozomi Networks

## RSA 2026
- **Booth number and hall:** Unknown.
- **Any updated booth info from the conference floor:** No specific updated booth information from the conference floor is available at this time.

## Company Profile
- **Employee count (approximate):** Nozomi Networks has approximately 383 employees as of February 2026. Other sources indicate around 378, 385, 365, or 340 employees.
- **Founded year:** 2013.
- **HQ location:** San Francisco, California, USA.
- **Funding stage or public status / ARR if known:** Nozomi Networks was acquired by Mitsubishi Electric Corporation on January 28, 2026, and now operates as a wholly-owned subsidiary. Prior to the acquisition, it was venture capital-backed, having raised a total of $266 million across 8 funding rounds, with its latest being a Series E round in March 2024. Another source indicates total funding of $869.47 million. The company recently surpassed $100 million in annual revenue.
- **Website URL:** www.nozominetworks.com

## Technology Stack
- **Core technology and architecture:** Nozomi Networks provides an AI-powered platform for OT (Operational Technology), ICS (Industrial Control Systems), and IoT (Internet of Things) security. Its architecture combines network (wired and wireless) and endpoint visibility with continuous monitoring and AI-powered analysis. The platform utilizes various sensors, including Guardian (for wired network visibility), Arc (for endpoint visibility), Guardian Air (for wireless spectrum visibility), Arc Embedded (an embedded sensor inside ICS endpoints), and Remote Collector (for remote locations). It employs passive and active data collection techniques, deep packet inspection (DPI), and comprehensive protocol fluency to analyze network traffic and understand behavior. The platform offers both cloud-based (Vantage) and on-premise (Central Management Console) centralized management options.
- **AI/ML frameworks or capabilities used:** Nozomi Networks integrates a variety of AI and machine learning (ML) models throughout its platform, including predictive analysis, behavioral analytics, Bayesian Networks, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Generative AI, custom-trained models, clustering, neural networks, and time-series analysis. These are used for automatic asset identification and classification, advanced vulnerability assessments, adaptive baselining, process anomaly detection, precise threat detection, alert correlation, root-cause analysis, and dynamic risk calculations.
